mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and household spending, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2010 payrolls at 129526 thousand, recovering from recession lows
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy expectations that compress equity risk premiums, causing the S&P 500 to produce positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE > 9.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) captures a broad-based recovery in both output and employment that single-series signals miss, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO x PAYEMS) exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are not significantly higher during joint-up months than during single-up months.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly signals consumer demand strength without being offset by tighter monetary policy, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS < 0.25% regimes, RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to achieve a positive Rank IC (IC <= 0) for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reduces short-term funding costs for financial intermediaries and corporate borrowers, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following 3-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units, the probability of further fiscal or monetary stimulus rises, and defensive positioning in equities reverses, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as stimulus expectations are priced in.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 600, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) during a low-inflation period reduces real purchasing power and raises input cost concerns for corporations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL increases > 0.2% are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals the persistence of aggregate demand expansion, which reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2009 vintage GDP at 13161.1 billion chained 2017 dollars, indicating recovery from Q2 2009 trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
